items.name: Cooling Tower
items.description: Cycles water to cool through contact with air.

items.name: Energy
items.description:
Stored in a high-capacity battery. Quantity is measured in kilowatt-hours (kWh).

A fully charged battery is enough to provide backup power for a city while the main supply is repaired. *IRL Note: Present-day batteries cannot hold such amounts.*

items.name: Funds
items.description: Money is required for research & development.

items.name: Lab
items.description: Desks, wires, and tools clutter this area.

items.name: Offices
items.description: More offices makes it easier to have more employees.

items.name: Plutonium
items.description:
Valuable nuclear material used in a reactor to generate high energy levels.

Measured in units of 100 μg (0.0001 g). This amount causes 1e17 fission reactions, generating 1 kWh of energy.

items.name: Plutonium Purchase I
items.description: +1 to how much fuel is available per production cycle.

items.name: Production Rate I
items.description: +1 each to energy production rate. This does not improve efficiency, so also increase the waste generated and resources required.

items.name: Production Rate II
items.description:
Energy production rate × (4 per) for a fairly steep linear increase. This does not improve efficiency, so also increase the waste generated and resources required.

Producing the first increase makes an uneven 1.25 — edit this item and set quantity to 1 for quadruple effect.

items.name: Radioactive Contamination
items.description:
There's really no way to get rid of it other than to wait for materials to dissipate. Requires full evacuation, unless you want to roll and see which workers survive.

- Set quantity to max.
- Produce until quantity returns to 0, then you may resume normal plant operation.

items.name: Radioactive Waste
items.description:
Glowing, greenish sludge. Stored in thick metal barrels—just hope they don't spring a leak.

Can be consumed and neutralized by bio-engineered bacteria.

items.name: Reactor
items.description: Accelerates atomic particles to collide with nuclei, splitting them into smaller parts and releasing energy.

items.name: Spent Fuel
items.description: Nuclear fuel becomes inefficient and radioactive after use. Refine into fresh plutonium by atomic reclamation.

items.name: Thermal Waste
items.description: Generated heat could turn nearby rivers into hot springs, upsetting 🐟 fishes.

items.name: Workers
items.description: Keep track of how many employees work here and their average mood. If their mood or health are bad then they may consider leaving.

locations.name: Energy Core Room
locations.description: Large chamber with a controlled environment where fission takes place.

locations.name: Outdoor Facility
locations.description: Handle excess heat away from the main room.

locations.name: Research and Development
locations.description: Design and build upgrades to improve production.

attribs.name: Layoffs
attribs.description: How many total workers were just let go.

attribs.name: Mood
attribs.description:
Scale of 1 (miserable) to 10 (highly motivated to work).

- Injury lowers mood based on how many workers get injured.
- Long hours and poor pay negatively affect mood.

attribs.name: Pay
attribs.description: Roughly keep track of how pay compares to industry averages.

events.name: Plant Problem Type
events.description:
Get more information about a problem that occurred. Possible outcomes:

- **Unusable Amenities** — employees don't appreciate this kind of work environment
- **Low Wages** — employees are unhappy and may go on strike
- **Waste Bacteria Inert** — fail to reduce radioactive waste
- **Radioactive Waste Leak** — significantly increases amount of radioactive waste (cleanup becomes a bigger job)
- **Cooling System Leak** — less efficient thermal waste reduction, could potentially hit employees with scalding water
- **Mechanical Failure** — temporarily prevents some systems from operating (3 production cycles), may require mechanical repairs
- **Electricity Down** — temporarily prevents some systems from operating (3 production cycles), may require repairs to electrical system
- **Energy Not Gained** — nothing to fix but roll back recent energy gains
- **Excess Energy** — this is a bigger problem, damaging storage systems
- **Battery Power Loss** — reduce [stored energy quantity](/configure/item/9) by 5%
- **Core Meltdown — Reactor Damage** — cannot produce energy until [repairs are made](/play/item/6)
- **Core Meltdown — Radioactive Contamination** — worst-case scenario, [wait for it to dissipate](/play/item/29)

If the inciting roll was a major failure then consider the problem to be more severe.
events.selection_strings:
Radioactive Waste Leak
Waste Bacteria Inert
Cooling System Leak
Mechanical Failure
Electricity Down
Unusable Amenities
Energy Not Gained
Excess Energy
Battery Power Loss
Core Meltdown — Reactor Damage
Core Meltdown — Radioactive Contamination

events.name: Production Rate
events.description: Determine production rate by taking upgrades into account.

events.name: Reclaim Spent Fuel
events.description:
- Waste buildup increases the chance of something going wrong.
- Sets **Spent Fuel** to 0 and set plutonium quantity to that amount instead, plus 25% on major success.
- If there is a failure then you still get the reclaimed fuel, but something goes wrong—determine **Plant Problem Type**.

events.name: Repair Reactor
events.description:
Not cheap. Costs more for more advanced equipment. May have to go into debt if you don't have the funds available, or choose not to restore all upgrades yet.

Deduct this results from [funds quantity](/configure/item/11), then you may use the reactor again.

events.name: Workers: Fire
events.description:
Lower selected worker counts and set Layoffs to how many you just removed total.

If the outcome is successful, then the layoffs increase profits, resulting in a [pay raise](/play/attrib/24/subject/19).

events.name: Workers: Retain
events.description: If they're not happy then they may decide to leave.

overall settings.title: Fission Plant
overall settings.description: Produce enough energy to fill a high-capacity battery while managing {orange|waste}. Handle any critical failures and then restore operations.
